steps.title: Recognizing equivalent equations
steps.content: For infinitely many solutions, both equations must represent the same line. The second equation must be equivalent to $y = 6x + 3$ — they should simplify to the same form.

steps.title: Simplify each choice
steps.content:
• A) $y = 2(6x) + 3 = 12x + 3$ — different slope, not equivalent.
  • B) $y = 2(6x+3) = 12x + 6$ — different slope and intercept, not equivalent.
  • C) $2y = 2(6x) + 3 = 12x + 3$, so $y = 6x + 1.5$ — different intercept, not equivalent.
  • D) $2y = 2(6x+3) = 12x + 6$, so $y = 6x + 3$ — this matches the original equation exactly. D is the original equation multiplied by 2 on both sides, which preserves equivalence. That matches choice D — D is correct.
